figures.gloss: An esoteric system formalized by Clarence 13X where the digits 0-9 correspond to immutable divine realities (e.g., 1 is Knowledge, 7 is God, 9 is Born). It is the structural code used to read the universe.
figures.facet: Numbers are not mere quantitative tools but the literal, active building blocks of consciousness. Mastering them allows the initiate to 'show and prove' their own divinity in everyday street life.
figures.provenance: Teachings of Clarence 13X and the Five-Percent Nation (1960s).

figures.gloss: The visionary founder of the Moorish Science Temple who taught that Black Americans are 'Asiatics' and 'Moors' descended from Moroccans, bearing a suppressed Islamic heritage and a sovereign nationality.
figures.facet: He subverted the American racial caste system by offering an esoteric civic identity, replacing the socially subjugated label 'Negro' with an ancient, noble Moorish nationality rooted in divine law.
figures.provenance: 'Holy Koran of the Moorish Science Temple of America' (Circle Seven Koran, 1927).

figures.gloss: The enlightened 5% of humanity who know the truth of the Original Man and teach it, standing against the 85% who are blind and the 10% who hoard knowledge to exploit the masses.
figures.facet: It creates a strict epistemological caste system based entirely on esoteric awakening rather than wealth, race, or birth, placing the burden of cosmic salvation on a highly disciplined, street-level vanguard.
figures.provenance: The 120 Lessons; Teachings of the Five-Percent Nation.

figures.gloss: An acronym interpreting the very anatomy of the Black man as the living manifestation of the Supreme Being. The physical body itself is the sacred geometry of the divine.
figures.facet: It completely collapses theology into anthropology. There is no 'mystery God' in the sky; divinity is achieved and localized entirely within the realized human form.
figures.provenance: Teachings of Clarence 13X and the Five-Percent Nation (1960s).

figures.gloss: Born Clarence Edward Smith, a former Nation of Islam minister at Temple No. 7 in Harlem who left the movement in 1963-64 over the divinity teaching's application and founded the Five Percenters (Nation of Gods and Earths), teaching that the Black man collectively — not one embodied peddler — is Allah. He was assassinated in Harlem in 1969 and is revered by Five Percenters as Allah the Father.
figures.facet: He converts Fard's singular, vanished God-in-a-body into a distributed, everyday divinity: any righteous Black man can 'show and prove' his own godhood on the street corner, no temple hierarchy required.
figures.provenance: Michael Muhammad Knight, 'The Five Percenters: Islam, Hip-Hop, and the Gods of New York' (2007); NYPD and press accounts of the 1969 killing.

figures.gloss: The companion cipher to the Supreme Mathematics: the letters A through Z assigned fixed divine meanings (A = Allah, B = Be or Born, C = See, and so on), used to 'break down' words into their hidden layers of sense.
figures.facet: Language itself becomes revelatory technology — an English word is not arbitrary sound but a coded sentence in the Alphabet, so reading correctly is itself an act of divine perception.
figures.provenance: Michael Muhammad Knight, 'The Five Percenters' (2007); Supreme Alphabet lesson sheets as transmitted in Five Percent oral/print culture since the mid-1960s.

figures.gloss: The catechism inherited from the Nation of Islam's Lost-Found Muslim Lessons — Student Enrollment, Lesson No. 1 and No. 2, Actual Facts, English Lesson C1 — memorized question-and-answer by Clarence 13X at Temple No. 7 before 1964, and carried into Five-Percent practice as the culture's core scripture, recited degree by degree.
figures.facet: The same catechism does two jobs depending on who holds it: disciplinary doctrine inside the registered Temple, cipher-recited street scripture inside the Five Percent — one text, two regimes of transmission.
figures.provenance: Michael Muhammad Knight, 'The Five Percenters' (2007); the Lessons as catalogued in NOI/Five-Percent lesson-sheet culture.

figures.gloss: In Nation of Gods and Earths doctrine the Black woman is the Earth, the necessary complement to the man who is God/Sun; she is taught to cover three-quarters of her body as the physical Earth is three-quarters water, and to raise the Original Seed (children) in the culture's teachings.
figures.facet: Cosmology is mapped directly onto gender role: astronomy (Sun/Earth ratio) becomes the literal warrant for a domestic order, divinity for the man and stewardship of the home and the young for the woman.
figures.provenance: Michael Muhammad Knight, 'The Five Percenters' (2007), on Five Percent gender doctrine and its internal contestation.

edges.note: Intra-tradition bond: Clarence 13X bridged the NOI concept of God from a specific historical figure (Fard) to the universalized, awakened Black man (A.L.L.A.H.).
Intra-tradition bond: Fard historically absorbed and mutated the esoteric 'Asiatic' identity and Moorish cosmological frameworks established earlier by Noble Drew Ali.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Five-Percent Nation's mathematics mirrors Hermeticism: every single physical number and letter corresponds flawlessly to an active, cosmic spiritual principle.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent a comprehensive, revealed system of interpreting mundane physical data to decode the explicit will and order of the cosmos.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Visionary syncretists who explicitly drew from diverse regional faiths to synthesize an entirely new, culturally specific esoteric identity and scripture.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both systems completely anthropomorphize cosmic reality, mapping the fundamental shape and potential of the universe directly onto the idealized human anatomy.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The 'Arm Leg Leg Arm Head' doctrine conceptualizes the Supreme Being structurally as a Cosmic Man, much like the Vedic Purusha whose body comprises the universe.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The enlightened Five Percent operate as the realized fullness of divine truth within an ignorant world, holding the cosmological knowledge required to restore universal balance.
Same historical person: Noble Drew Ali is carried in the NOI-esoteric shard as an ancestor-figure of the Five-Percent lineage; this shard is his home tradition.
Clarence 13X formalized the Supreme Mathematics as the numeric cipher of the culture he founded.
The Alphabet is the letter-cipher companion to his Mathematics, taught together from the mid-1960s.
Inseparable companion ciphers: numbers and letters together are the full code for 'showing and proving.'
He founded the Five Percent Nation that this vanguard concept names.
His teaching that the Black man collectively is Allah is the doctrinal ground the A.L.L.A.H. formula anatomizes.
He memorized the Lessons as a registered Temple No. 7 member before departing to re-teach their content in cipher form.
Man-as-God and Woman-as-Earth are the culture's paired cosmic complements.
His own scripture (home tradition: Moorish Science), the textual root that this shard's Five-Percent/esoteric lineage inherits and re-cites.
(restated for the Alphabet specifically): a comprehensive, revealed code for reading hidden divine sense into ordinary language, paralleling the Mathematics' reading of numbers.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: both are memorized catechetical scriptures assembled by a single teacher and transmitted as the closed core text of the movement.

The same underlying catechism (the Lost-Found Muslim Lessons dictated by Fard to Elijah Muhammad) inherited and re-transmitted two ways: as registered NOI doctrine here, and as Five-Percent cipher-recitation in the esoteric shard.

dossier.essence: An umbrella for the esoteric currents of Black-American Islam that run alongside and out of the Nation of Islam — principally Noble Drew Ali's Moorish Science Temple (founded 1913) and the Five-Percent Nation, or Nation of Gods and Earths, founded in Harlem in 1964 by Clarence 13X ('Allah the Father') after he left the NOI. Their shared claim is that the Black ('Asiatic') man is the original and divine being, and that liberation comes through recovering suppressed esoteric knowledge of that identity.
dossier.cosmos: These currents share the NOI's mytho-history — the Original Man as maker and owner of the planet, Yakub's engineering of the white race, the coming end of the wicked world aboard the Mother Plane — but push its logic toward an internalized, decoded divinity. In Five-Percent teaching God is not a mystery in the sky but the Black man himself (A.L.L.A.H. = 'Arm, Leg, Leg, Arm, Head'), and reality is made legible through Supreme Mathematics and the Supreme Alphabet, systems mapping the digits 0–9 and the letters onto fixed cosmic principles. Humanity divides into the deceived 85%, the exploiting 10%, and the enlightened 5% ('poor righteous teachers') who know and must teach the truth.
dossier.practice: Moorish Science gave adherents a reclaimed 'nationality' (Moorish/Asiatic), names ending in -El or -Bey, the fez, and Friday temple meetings. The Five-Percent Nation is largely non-institutional and street-based: members ('Gods' and 'Earths') memorize and 'build' on the 120 Lessons, Supreme Mathematics, and the Supreme Alphabet, teaching through cyphers rather than clergy, and the movement deeply shaped hip-hop culture and vocabulary. Neither runs a centralized priesthood in the NOI's disciplined mold.
dossier.canon: Noble Drew Ali's 'Holy Koran of the Moorish Science Temple of America' (the 'Circle Seven Koran', 1927); for the Five Percent, the memorized '120 Lessons' (the NOI's Supreme Wisdom material), Supreme Mathematics, and the Supreme Alphabet, transmitted orally.
dossier.reflects: Divinity relocated into the self and the street — a gnosis in which cosmic truth is carried as memorized cipher and the initiate's own body and name become the proof of godhood.
sources: Michael Muhammad Knight, 'The Five Percenters: Islam, Hip-Hop and the Gods of New York' (2007)
Michael A. Gomez, 'Black Crescent: The Experience and Legacy of African Muslims in the Americas' (2005)
Peter Lamborn Wilson, 'Sacred Drift: Essays on the Margins of Islam' (1993)
